description: Comparison vs popular RAG frameworks on identical hardware and corpus

rag_engine.reranker: ONNX cross-encoder (domain fine-tuned) + late interaction pre-filter

langchain_rag.notes: FAISS index fully in RAM (no mmap). GIL contention visible above 200 QPS per replica. Cohere API adds network latency to reranking.

llamaindex.notes: Qdrant adds serialization overhead on the gRPC boundary. Python-native cross-encoder inference is the bottleneck at high QPS.

haystack.notes: Better throughput than LangChain/LlamaIndex thanks to Weaviate's internal batching. Still limited by Python orchestration layer.

why_rag_engine_wins.latency: Rust HNSW core with AVX2 SIMD does vector search outside the Python runtime, zero GIL contention. 4.2x faster distance computation than scalar. Memory-mapped index means no deserialization cost on startup.
why_rag_engine_wins.quality: HyDE for short queries + late interaction reranking + domain fine-tuned cross-encoder. Competitors use generic models or API-based rerankers with higher latency.
why_rag_engine_wins.cost: 8-10x cheaper per query because we need fewer replicas to hit the same QPS target. The Rust core with AVX2 processes 5x more queries per CPU core.
why_rag_engine_wins.memory: Memory-mapped HNSW index means only hot pages reside in RAM. Competitors load full indices into heap memory.
